prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for furan computed via DFT at the tier standard level (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, exact integrals, no density fitting) in PySCF. Gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet); cation and anion states (both doublets) computed at the same level for Fukui index derivation via Mulliken charges. All three SCF calculations converged. Charge conservation checks confirmed (sum f+ = 1.0000, sum f- = 1.0000).

The most electrophilic site is carbon at position 4 (one of the two ring carbons beta to oxygen), with f+ = +1.924. The most nucleophilic site is also the same carbon, with f− = +0.162. The oxygen atom shows strong resistance to electrophilic attack (f+ = −1.332) consistent with its high electronegativity. The ring carbons adjacent to oxygen (positions 3 and 4) are activated toward nucleophilic attack, while hydrogens are unreactive. A PNG bar chart visualization has been generated showing f+, f−, and dual descriptor per atom. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy = true. No warnings reported beyond routine convergence checks.
